highlights:
do you know about the medical care afforded by the Khmer kings particularly Jayavarman VII?
...Read more ## Key takeaways
* In every hospital compound, there's a temple. * So, there was approximately 90 to 98 people for a single hospital.  ...    * Neak Pean (or Neak Poan "the entwined serpents") at Angkor, Cambodia is an artificial island with a Buddhist temple on a circular island in Jayatataka Baray, which was associated with Preah Khan temple, built during the reign of King Jayavarman VII.  ...    King Jayavarman VII of the Khmer Empire built a well-supplied, country-wide hospital network, open to all citizens, some 250 years before Europe's first hospice for the poor was founded at Beaune in France in 1443. After his violent accession to the throne, it was surely a stroke of political genius for Jayavarman to deploy Buddhist compassion and the Master of Medicines Buddha for legitimating his rule and healing the wounds of civil war.
